mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month when the policy rate trend is falling rather than flat or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline during periods when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, or if the sign of the effect turns negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recessionary labor-market threshold signals deteriorating household income and weaker earnings expectations, so S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next month once unemployment is elevated and still rising.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances with UNRATE above 9.5% produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A strong month-over-month increase in retail sales relative to the recent trend indicates broad demand resilience, supporting near-term corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average by 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales breakout events do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears after controlling for recent market momentum in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while CPIAUCSL remains contained, the market can infer real activity is strengthening without immediate inflation pressure, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through better margins and earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and stable CPIAUCSL does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in the top-quartile interaction bucket.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST signals weakening construction demand and poorer cyclically sensitive earnings, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next month when housing starts break materially below recent levels.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average by 10%
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts breakdowns are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns with frequency above 55%, or if the signal has no explanatory power after accounting for broad labor-market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, labor income growth and confidence improve together, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not improve 60-day SPX hit rate relative to either labor series alone, or if the interaction coefficient is not stable across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, CPIAUCSL acceleration above policy-rate growth forces real-rate expectations higher and compresses equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days after inflation re-accelerates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds FEDFUNDS by 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-over-policy crossings are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the signal weakens materially once the policy rate is already near zero.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter rise in GDPC1 after a recessionary low indicates a genuine demand rebound that supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, especially when monthly activity data are also improving.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 turns positive versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 turnarounds do not lead to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is not stronger when INDPRO and RSAFS are simultaneously improving.
